channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Intune Data Platform Schema - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the Microsoft Intune data platform schema for device queries and inventory. Discover supported properties and data types to efficiently manage and report on your devices with Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune documentation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage devices, apps, and security with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use Intune Suite add-on capabilities - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune Suite unifies a series of mission-critical advanced endpoint management and security capabilities. The capabilities of the suite are integrated with Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Security across endpoint platforms for both cloud and on-premises co-managed devices.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Operating systems and browsers supported by Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Lists supported device platforms and browsers for Intune device management

item.title: Licenses available for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune is available with these licenses

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidelines for using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Migration guide to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deployment guide to set up, onboard, or move to Intune. These steps include moving from partner MDM providers, using co-management, moving from on-premises group policy, and moving from Office 365 device management.

item.title: Tutorial - Walkthrough the Microsoft Intune Admin Center - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial, you will tour Microsoft Intune in the Microsoft Intune admin center to better understand how to accomplish tasks.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune provides specific report types with focused views that contain consistent and timely data.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Get started with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See an overview of the steps to start using Intune. Plan your move and deployment of Intune, determine your licensing needs and any platform requirements, use compliance and Conditional Access, deploy apps, create device configuration profiles, and enroll your devices to be managed. Get more information on mobile application management for BYOD or personal devices.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: A guide to managing iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage and secure devices overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing devices that access organization resources in Microsoft Intune. You can manage new and existing devices, including BYOD personal devices, check health compliance and view reports, configure device features, and secure devices using mobile threat solutions.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Join your cloud-native endpoints to Microsoft Entra - Microsoft Intune
item.description: When moving to or using cloud-native endpoints, use Microsoft Entra joined endpoints. When your endpoints are joined to Microsoft Entra, you can use Windows Autopilot to provision or get devices ready for organization use. Learn more about the benefits to IT admins and end-users.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Government Service overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Intune government service offerings and features. This article is designed to serve as an overview of the Microsoft Intune offering for government community cloud (GCC) High and United States Department of Defense (DoD) environments.

item.title: Manage and secure user and group identities overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing identities in Microsoft Intune. Use existing users and groups, control access using RBAC, establish user affinity, and secure and authenticate users.

item.title: Add, Configure, and Protect Apps with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, and protect apps with Intune.

item.title: Zero Trust with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune contributes to a strong Zero Trust strategy and architecture by managing and securing endpoints that access organizational resources.

item.title: Startup Performance Report in Endpoint Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how startup performance in Microsoft Intune endpoint analytics helps analyze boot times, identify issues, and optimize device performance.

item.title: Configure compliance policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Description for configuring compliance policies

item.title: Network endpoints for US government deployments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See the list of US government endpoint URLs that Intune needs and requires. Allow the ports, IP addresses, and endpoint URLs in your proxy server configuration.

item.title: Deployment guide for Linux device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Linux device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Policy Sets - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use policy sets to group collections of management objects in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Get started with macOS endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune guide to set up and configure macOS devices from setup to creating policies and enrolling devices. You secure your corporate and organization-owned endpoints that are enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager.

item.title: Endpoint management services and solutions at Microsoft - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune is a family of on-premises products and cloud services. It includes Intune, Configuration Manager, co-management, Endpoint Analytics, Windows Autopilot, and the admin center to manage cloud devices and on on-premises.

item.title: Deployment guide for Windows device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Windows device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Protection and configuration levels overview in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the different levels of protection and configuration in Microsoft Intune, including minimum, enhanced, and high levels. Understand the recommended policies for apps, compliance, and device configuration at each level to secure your organization's resources effectively.

item.title: Centrally manage Admin Tasks - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Centrally manage admin tasks in Microsoft Intune. Use the unified Admin tasks view to organize and act on administrative tasks from Device Offboarding, Endpoint Privilege Management, and more.

item.title: Help Desk Troubleshooting Dashboard - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Help desk staff use the troubleshooting pane to solve users' technical problems.

item.title: Distributed IT with multiple admins - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about scaling guidelines for Microsoft Intune when you have a large number of local admins who need to manage their own users/devices and policies within the same tenant. Use Microsoft Intune's Role Based Access Control to manage access.

item.title: Manage device operating system versions with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the methods for managing device operating system versions supported by Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure security, email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 4 to deploy device configuration profiles as part of the minimum set of policies for your devices using Microsoft Intune. The starting point is to enable the firewall, install AV, scan for malware, install software updates, create a strong PIN policy, and create email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Service Description - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune is a cloud-based service that helps you manage Windows, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, and Android devices.

item.title: Zero Trust deployment approach with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended seven-layer deployment progression for implementing Zero Trust device security with Microsoft Intune, from app protection to endpoint data loss prevention.

item.title: Update your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To support hybrid and remote workers, convert or migrate your workloads to support cloud-native endpoints. This planning guide focuses on deploying apps and updates with Intune, moving from Group Policy Objects, and using Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Windows cloud configuration step by step guide - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use this Windows 10/11 cloud configuration step-by-step setup guide to create your own cloud configuration deployment. You create the Microsoft Entra group and policies using Microsoft Intune, including the enrollment profile, compliance policy, and security baseline. You also deploy apps and resources that users need to do their jobs.

item.title: Tutorial-Get started with cloud-native Windows endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up secure cloud-native Windows endpoints that are Microsoft Entra joined, enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Remote Device Actions - Wipe, Lock, Locate, and More - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how to use Microsoft Intune to remotely manage, wipe, lock, restart, and secure Android, iOS/iPadOS, macOS, Windows, and ChromeOS devices. Learn about available remote actions, prerequisites, and bulk actions for IT admins.

item.title: Move from Basic Mobility and Security to Intune-Migration Guide - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Follow this migration guide to move your mobile device management from Microsoft 365 Basic Mobility and Security to Intune. Includes policy mapping and license assignment steps.

item.title: Apple configuration list for Intune settings catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune settings catalog to add, configure, or restrict features on Apple devices. This article lists and describes the settings you can configure.

item.title: Explore Intune data with natural language and take action - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In Microsoft Intune, use Explorer to query your data in natural language and run built-in queries that match your request. Copilot summarizes the results, and provides recommendations and actions based on the query results. You can also create policies that target users and groups in the query results. Use this feature to explore your Intune data, troubleshoot issues, and create custom reports.

item.title: Use Windows Holographic devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage and complete different tasks on devices running Windows Holographic for Business and HoloLens. You can configure the Company Portal app, create a compliance policy, customize OMA-URI settings, deploy apps, categorize devices in groups, create profiles, restrict devices, enable software updates, set terms and conditions, configure VPN and Wi-Fi settings, and use Hello for Business.

item.title: Compliance in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about compliance, dependencies, and features in Microsoft Intune supporting data protection and regulatory requirements.

item.title: Use imported PFX certificates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use imported Public Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) certificates with Microsoft Intune. Import certificates, configure certificate templates, and create an Imported PKCS Certificate profile.

item.title: How to Use Microsoft Entra ID to Access Intune APIs in Microsoft Graph - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Describes steps needed for apps to use Microsoft Entra ID to access the Intune APIs in Microsoft Graph.

item.title: Enroll a Windows device - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to enroll a Windows device into Microsoft Intune. Follow this step-by-step evaluation guide to test device enrollment and verify it in the Intune admin center.

item.title: Use Shell Scripts on macOS Devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create, assign, monitor, and troubleshoot shell scripts for macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Learn about using Intune to manage Microsoft Defender settings on devices that aren't enrolled with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Intune policy to manage Microsoft Defender security settings on devices that aren't enrolled with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use CrowdStrike Falcon for Mobile with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to set up CrowdStrike Falcon Threat Defense with Microsoft Intune control mobile device access to your corporate resources.

item.title: Set up Better Mobile integration with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Integrate the third-party mobile threat defense solution of Better Mobile with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Learn about Intune security baselines for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that have preset and recommended configurations to the Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS personal device security configurations - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review example personal device security configurations of basic, enhanced, and high security for iOS devices.

item.title: Deploy Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand the steps and phases for deploying Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use EPM support approvals for file elevation requests with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage support approvals for elevation requests when you use Endpoint Privilege Management for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Review the type of data that Microsoft Intune Endpoint Privilege Management collects - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View details about the type of data Endpoint Privilege Management can collect and store when used with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure compliance policies with actions for noncompliance in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure your compliance policies with one or more actions for noncompliance to protect devices and your organization from unprotected devices. Actions can remotely lock devices, send email or notifications to device users, and more.

item.title: Manage Outlook for iOS and Android With Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune app protection and configuration policies with Outlook for iOS and Android to ensure team collaboration experiences are always accessed with safeguards in place.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Tunnel for MAM iOS SDK Developer Guide - Microsoft Intune
item.description: The Microsoft Tunnel for MAM iOS SDK lets you incorporate support for Mobile Application Management (MAM) Tunnel into your native iOS app. iOS/iPadOS app developers should use this article to integrate per-app VPN when they're creating and developing line of business (LOB) apps.

item.title: Manage antivirus settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure and deploy policies and use reports for devices you manage with endpoint security antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Encrypt Windows devices with BitLocker using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to manage BitLocker encryption on Windows devices, including silent encryption and Personal Data Encryption.

item.title: Manage endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Security Administrators can use the Endpoint Security policies and profiles to focus on security configuration of devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Intune endpoint security firewall settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Endpoint security firewall policy settings for Windows and macOS in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Windows Antivirus policy settings from Windows security settings for tenant attached devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the settings in the *Windows Security experience* profile for tenant attached devices. To use this Endpoint security Antivirus policy, you must first configure tenant attach for Configuration Manager for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Set up Android (AOSP) device management in Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) platform.

item.title: Create discovery scripts for custom compliance policy in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create scripts for Linux or Windows devices to discover the settings you define as custom compliance settings for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Monitor results of your device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device compliance dashboard to understand overall device compliance the per policy and per setting device compliance results.

item.title: Use app-based Conditional Access policies with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about using app-based Conditional Access policies with Microsoft Intune for both enrolled and unenrolled devices.

item.title: Use Conditional Access with Microsoft Intune compliance policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Combine Conditional Access with Intune compliance policies to define the requirements that users and devices must meet before gaining access your organizations resources.

item.title: Install the Microsoft Tunnel VPN for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Install and configure Microsoft Tunnel Gateway on a Linux server to support Intune managed cloud-based devices in connecting to your on-premises infrastructure.

item.title: Prerequisites the Microsoft Tunnel VPN for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the prerequisites for installation and use of the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. These include Linux servers, network, and firewall configurations.

item.title: File and command reference for Microsoft Tunnel Gateway, a VPN solution for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find file and command-line references for tools you use to install or manage the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway, a VPN server that runs on Linux.

item.title: Create Mobile Threat Defense compliance policies in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create an Intune device compliance policy that uses your MTD partner threat levels to determine if a mobile device can access company resources.

item.title: Set up Jamf Mobile Security with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to set up Jamf Mobile Threat Defense with Microsoft Intune to control mobile device access to your corporate resources.

item.title: Set up Lookout Mobile Endpoint Security with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to set up Lookout Mobile Endpoint Security and Microsoft Intune to control mobile device access to your corporate resources.

item.title: Set up Sophos Mobile integration with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to set up the Sophos Mobile solution with Microsoft Intune to control mobile device access to your corporate resources.

item.title: Monitor security baselines deployed by Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Monitor device and per-setting results of security baselines you deploy with Microsoft Intune, and identify conflicts for devices.

item.title: Managing Elevation Settings for Endpoint Privilege Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View guidance on how to manage the Endpoint Privilege Management client, including reporting level and default elevation response.

item.title: How to Monitor App Protection Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes how to monitor app protection policies in Intune.

item.title: Configure device configuration profiles in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add or configure a device configuration profile in Microsoft Intune. Select the platform type, configure the settings, and add a scope tag. Learn more about applicability rules, and the policy refresh cycle times.

item.title: Manage approved apps for Windows devices with App Control for Business policy and Managed Installers in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use App Control for Business policies and a managed installer to manage which apps are approved to run on Windows devices that you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Create a JSON file for custom compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create the JSON file that defines custom settings and values for use with device compliance policies in Intune.

item.title: Android (AOSP) compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for Android (AOSP) that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Windows 8.1 compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for Windows 8.1 that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Monitor the Microsoft Tunnel VPN solution for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Monitor the status of Microsoft Tunnel Gateway, a VPN server that runs on Linux. Microsoft Tunnel enables your Intune managed cloud-based devices to reach your on-premises infrastructure.

item.title: Set up BlackBerry Protect with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to set up the CylancePROTECT (BlackBerry) MTD solution with Microsoft Intune to control mobile device access to your corporate resources.

item.title: Settings list for the Microsoft Intune security baseline for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the settings in the Microsoft Intune security baseline for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and each settings default value.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS device compliance security configurations - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review example device compliance configurations of basic, enhanced, and high security for iOS devices.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS supervised device security configurations - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review example supervised device security configurations of basic, enhanced, and high security for iOS devices.

item.title: Admin checklist for iOS/iPadOS software updates in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidance and advice for administrators that create and manage software updated for iOS/iPadOS devices using Microsoft Intune. See sample policy for different industry scenarios, including shared devices, kiosk, manufacturing, and information worker.

item.title: Step 3. Integrate Mobile Threat Defense for App Protection Policy - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 3. Integrate Mobile Threat Defense signals with Microsoft Edge for Business app protection policies in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Tutorial - Configure Slack to Use Intune for EMM and App Configuration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial you will configure Slack to use Intune for EMM and app configuration.

item.title: Microsoft Security Copilot agents in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Security Copilot agents in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Encrypt macOS devices with FileVault using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to configure and manage FileVault disk encryption on macOS devices, including Setup Assistant enforcement and comprehensive recovery key management.

item.title: Windows Antivirus policy settings from Microsoft Defender Antivirus for tenant attached devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of the settings in the Microsoft Defender Antivirus profile for Windows devices managed by Configuration Manager. You can configure these settings as part of Endpoint security Antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune after you configure tenant attach for Configuration Manager.

item.title: Intune endpoint security disk encryption policy settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the list of settings that are available in Microsoft Intune endpoint security disk encryption policy settings for BitLocker and FileVault.

item.title: Configure endpoint protection on macOS devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune to configure macOS devices use the built-in firewall to allow or block specific apps or to use stealth mode, to use Gatekeeper to determine where apps install, and to use FileVault disk encryption.
